eldora.note: 5:42 AM update – Good morning, skiers and riders! Welcome to the Eldora snow report for Tuesday, March 24th, 2026. Happy Tuesday, everyone! Come on up and enjoy some prime spring skiing conditions! The snow will be soft, there will be zero lift lines, and the vibes will be through the roof. Weekdays are the best days to come up to Eldora, and you'll realize why after a day of skiing and riding here. Make sure to have as much fun as possible out there! We can’t wait to see you out there today, and let’s have a great week! NWS Weather Snapshot: Mostly sunny, with a high near 63. Breezy, with a west wind 11 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph. 2026-27 season passes are now on sale . Lock in your lowest price now, before prices increase on April 30. Uphill and Nordic Skiing: The Nordic Center is now closed for the season. Check Nordic Pulse for the latest update. Uphill access is open, subject to the policies described at eldora.com/uphill . Reminder: Spring Hours are now in effect through Closing Day. Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week Stay in the loop: This page, eldora.com/conditions , is always your go-to for the latest updates, but you can also sign up for text alerts so you never miss a beat. Text one of the following keywords to (866) 965-6224: ELDORA for general alerts, UPHILL for uphill-specific updates, or NORDIC for Nordic updates. Message and data rates may apply. Thanks for checking the Eldora snow report. We'll see you on the hill! -- GENERAL INFORMATION Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week. Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week. Single-Occupancy Vehicle (SOV) Fees: Vehicles with two or more occupants may park for free seven days a week. Vehicles with one occupant will be charged $10 on weekends, during certain holiday periods, and on weekdays with 10 or more inches of new snow. Credit/debit is accepted; cash is not. High-Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) Parking: Per Boulder County requirements, the Elk Lot (nearest to Alpenglow chair) is reserved seven days a week for vehicles with three or more occupants on a first-come, first-served basis. Learn more here . EV Parking: Eldora provides 30 free ChargePoint EV charging stations. These premium parking spots are available on a first-come, first-served basis and are subject to the SOV policy above. To park in the EV lot, you MUST have an EV and be actively charging. If you are finished charging, please move your car to another non-EV space so others can use the chargers. Easy, Convenient RTD Bus Service from Boulder: RTD’s NB route brings you from Downtown Boulder Station straight to the Alpenglow chairlift in about 40 minutes; riders 19 and under ride RTD for FREE through RTD's Zero Fare for Youth program. For schedule and fare info, visit rtd-denver.com

killington.note: Good afternoon, Beast fans! Today saw a big improvement in our snow surfaces after our groomers worked some more magic, and tomorrow should bring more of the same sugary snow goodness on the slopes. We added a number of trails back to our count and are monitoring (and hoping for) some potential snow on Wednesday/Thursday. For tomorrow’s weather, we’re looking at temps starting in the high 20s and creeping up to the mid 30s by the time lifts stop spinning. Predominant cloud cover will return during the day, and stronger winds will open up the day before weakening a bit through the afternoon. Conditions out there today ranged from wintry in the morning to downright springy by the afternoon. My personal run of the day was Lower East Fall, which had some really fun snow that was soft but edge-able all the way down. I didn’t need to head to Bear Mountain to find the sun today, but I made a quick trip over anyway. Lower Wildfire had very soft snow in the sun, while Middle Superstar held more of its bite in the shade. It’s gonna be a while before that melts out! Our grooming plan will cover much of our open terrain again, with Lower Double Dipper and Downdraft getting swapped out for other steeper pitches like Panic Button and Lower Pipe Dream. Get in those South Ridge laps while you can! Wildfire and Skyeburst will also provide paths for cruising down to the Bear by the morning. For the full list of groomed options, head to our Lifts & Trails Report . There’s a small cold snap coming in this Saturday, so you know what that means: snowmaking is coming back, with around 10 of our fan guns laying down support on Lower Superstar, Mouse Trap, and areas right around our Superstar Umbrella Bar. It’s already spring, but we’re still prepping to keep winter alive as long as we can! Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open again tomorrow at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our Canyon and South Ridge Quads coming online at 10:00 a.m. In our kparks, the Bear parks (Dream Maker & The Stash) were back on the menu today for some sweet spring skiing & riding. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. Last thing: if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. See you tomorrow for more sugary laps! - Cullen Operating Day: 134 Lifts Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends. A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1! The Tubing Park presented by bubly is closed for the season. Uphill Travel Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific uphill route information, including a map of each route: Ramshead base to top of Ramshead Express Quad - Open Ramshead Express Quad to top of Snowdon Mountain - Open Snowshed base to the top of Snowshed Slope - Open Base of Sunrise Village Triple to top of Bear Mountain - Open Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect - CLOSED Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open Pico Summit Glade Route - CLOSED Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here . All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours. MOUNTAIN SERVICES Parking & Shuttles Parking is available at the K-1, Snowshed, Ramshead, Bear, Skyeship and Vale parking lots. Please respect signage and allow our parking staff to assist you on your trip to the slopes. Shuttle service is available from 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. midweek and 6:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends and holidays. Winter car camping and overnight parking is only available in the lot across from Skyeship Lodge. See details here . Lodge Services All lodges are open daily from 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekdays and 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends/peak dates*. K-1 Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. daily. Brew Bar (2nd floor): Open serving handcrafted coffees at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 7:30 a.m. on weekends, with cocktails and beers served starting at 9:00 a.m. K-1 Pub (3rd floor): Open at 10:00 a.m. daily. Rentals & Killington Sports at K-1: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Guest Services: 1st floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 1:00 p.m. on weekdays and until 4:30 p.m. on weekends/peak dates. 2nd floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Superstar Umbrella Bar: Open at 11:00 a.m. from Friday - Sunday offering beer, wine, liquor, and specialty hot cocktails. Snowshed Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. daily. Long Trail Pub (3rd floor): Open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., offering our Lunch Dining Menu until 5:30 p.m. and lite fare until 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Umbrella Bar: Open on Saturdays and Sundays serving beers, cocktails, and light fare. Rentals, Killington Sports at Snowshed, & Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. On Fridays, rentals & demos for the weekend can be picked up ahead of time between 2:00 p.m. and 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Demos & Tuning: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Ski & snowboard demo packages available. Please note that binding mounts and adjustments are not offered at this time. Click here for more information. Private & Adult Group Lessons: Booked online in advance here . Ramshead Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on Fridays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served daily with grab & go options & soups. The pizza station is open Thursday - Monday, and the full grill menu is available Friday - Sunday. Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 12:00 p.m. on Monday – Thursday and until 4:30 p.m. on Friday - Sunday at the ticket windows outside of the lodge. Rentals: Open for youth lesson participants and families only. Youth Lessons: Youth Group Lessons and our Ministars program meet at Ramshead. Child Care Center: Open weekdays from 8:30 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. and weekends from 8:00 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. Bear Mountain Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Open daily with grab & go items and soups available. Full grill menu available Friday - Sunday. Bear Bar: Open from 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m. on weekdays and until 5:00 p.m. on weekends. Guest Services: Available Friday - Monday at ticket windows outside the lodge. Open 30 minutes before lifts open. Kiosk lift ticket pickup is available daily. Skyeship Lodge Food & Bar Service: Available daily from The Rollin’ Rooster. Guest Services: Open daily inside the lodge. Closing Date: March 29th On-Mountain Dining Options Peak Lodge – Open daily from 9:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. Grill open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. Peak Lodge Bar offers its own menu daily with QR code ordering. Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill – Ski-on, ski- off. Open Thursday-Monday from 11:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. weather permitting. Ledgewood Yurt – closed for the season. Mountain services are subject to change. Please check here daily before making your lunch and lodge choices. Winter Lift Tickets and rentals must be purchased online at this link . Check to see if your pass is good to go using our Online Pass Checker . Find Season Pass FAQs by clicking here . Get your Unlimited Winter Season Pass today! Buy now . Have a ticketing question? Our text number ( 802-523-2202 ) is the best way to get it answered quickly, but please note that we cannot process transactions via text. Have a season pass question? Call 1-800-621-MTNS or email seasonpass@killington.com Please visit Killington.com/cancellation for more details on our cancellation policy. Killington Sports at Route 4 is open Sunday - Thursday from 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m., Friday and Saturday from 10:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., and 10:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. on holidays. Killington is a cashless resort. We accept all major credit cards as well as Apple, Google, and Samsung Pay. *No remaining Peak Dates during winter 2025/26. We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

copper mountain.note: Good morning from Copper Mountain, where we picked up an inch of fresh snow overnight at mid-mountain! At lower elevations we had a wintry mix, but surfaces should soften quickly, and the groomers should be super fun this morning. It'll be a classic spring day, with fresh snow and clouds giving way to sunshine and warm temperatures into the 50s. Tropical! Westerly winds 10-20 mph with gusts into the 40s will keep things feeling a little cooler up high. As always on these spring days, and especially with new snow, be prepared for changing conditions throughout the day. The trail you just rode might feel different on your second lap, so stay alert and adjust as you go. We're excited to see you out there and hope you have a fun day on the slopes!

snowbird.note: Resort Update: As of 3 pm today, Snowbird is closed for the day due to increasing wet slide activity. We appreciate your understanding. Due to warming and afternoon wet slide activity, Snowbird will operate 8 am - 2 pm with Chickadee running until 4 pm on Wednesday, March 25. Snow Report: Gooooood morning Snowbird! It’s Owen, with your snow report for Tuesday. We are back to our seemingly regularly scheduled program of sunny skies and warm temperatures today. We are going to have a high of around 60° and more slushy spring skiing conditions. With these conditions, now would be a good time to get those skis or boards waxed (I know you’ve been thinking about it). Go check out Cliff Sports for all your tuning needs, I just got mine done the other day - they'll get you right. ALL uphill travel is closed. Don’t be that guy (or gal). Please remember to ski and ride in control, if you don’t, you’re gonna have a bad time. That’s the snow report and if you don’t like it, too bad. I’ll be here all season. Except for Fridays and Saturdays - I’m off those days. #GMTB
